changed_file_regressions.message: `useEffect` can run with a stale `setLedgerNodes, setLedgerEdges` & show your users old data.
fetch() inside useEffect can race, double-fire, or leak. Use a data-fetching layer or Server Component instead.
`useEffect` can run with a stale `fetchRecords` & show your users old data.
This makes the for…of loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
`autoFocus` moves focus on load, which can disrupt screen reader and keyboard users. Remove it and let users choose where to focus.
This loops over your list twice because .filter().map() makes two passes, so do it in one pass with .reduce() or a for...of loop
Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "index".
"PromptCalibrationDashboard" updates 5 separate useState values in one place — state that changes together is easier to keep consistent as a single useReducer action.
This loops over your list twice because .map().filter(Boolean) makes two passes, so use .flatMap() to change & drop items in one pass
This scales poorly because `array.includes()` inside a loop scans the whole list every time. Use a Set for constant-time lookups.
This scales poorly because `array.includes()` inside a loop scans the whole list every time. Use a Set for constant-time lookups.
This loops over your list twice because .map().filter() makes two passes, so do it in one pass with .reduce() or a for...of loop

next_fix_queue.message: `useEffect` can run with a stale `setLedgerNodes, setLedgerEdges` & show your users old data.
Your effect re-subscribes whenever "onOpenChange" changes, even though it's only used inside `addEventListener`.
Your users briefly see stale state on every prop change.
Chaining state updates triggers an extra render each step.
fetch() inside useEffect can race, double-fire, or leak. Use a data-fetching layer or Server Component instead.
`useEffect` can run with a stale `fetchRecords` & show your users old data.
Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "index".
"PromptCalibrationDashboard" updates 5 separate useState values in one place — state that changes together is easier to keep consistent as a single useReducer action.
Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "index".
